directions.name: Replace the simple B-row skew with a warp-friendly shared-memory B swizzle
directions.hypothesis: The round 5 retile already pushed the kernel up to its current shared-memory occupancy ceiling: NCU reports 7 theoretical CTAs per SM from shared memory, 28 maximum warps per active cycle, and 58.03% active warps, so another occupancy-only tweak is no longer the first-order move. The remaining dominant stall is MIO throttle at 33.09%, and the detailed CSV still shows heavy LSU/L1 activity from the steady-state load path (`l1tex__data_pipe_lsu_wavefronts` 66.09%, `l1tex__data_bank_reads` 13.55%). Each warp still issues three `matrix_b` fragment loads per K-step from a row-major shared tile with only a +8-element skew, so a stronger B-side swizzle or permuted staging layout is the most direct way to reduce shared-load pressure and feed Tensor Cores more consistently.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Shared-memory and MIO pressure on the B fragment load path inside the steady-state tensor loop
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:36-44 (`kBSharedStride`, `kBSharedTileElems`, and B staging shape constants)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:204-211 (B shared pointer setup and `wmma::load_matrix_sync` for `b_frags`)
directions.risk: Moderate. The B layout change must preserve 16-byte `cp.async` alignment and the current WMMA row-major semantics; a swizzle that helps the warp-load pattern can still make staging math or correctness more fragile.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the swizzled B layout does not produce a clear drop in MIO throttle or LSU wavefront pressure, or if correctness becomes brittle enough that the extra shared indexing complexity is not paying for itself.

directions.name: Retune the cp.async pipeline so the 4-warp CTA pays fewer full-block wait/sync penalties
directions.hypothesis: The retiled CTA improved runtime substantially, but it also raised barrier stall to 23.82% because the mainloop still executes `cp_async_wait_group_0()` plus `__syncthreads()` around every 16-wide K slice. On this exact shape there is no tail work to hide, and the kernel already runs at the current 7-CTA shared-memory occupancy ceiling, so the next overlap win is likely to come from changing stage depth or phase ordering rather than more retile changes. A deeper ring buffer or a schedule that amortizes one block-wide wait across more useful MMA work should cut synchronization dead time without needing a new CTA shape.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Synchronization-limited overlap between async staging and MMA consumption in the steady-state mainloop
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:155-157 (double-buffered shared allocations for A and B)
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:182-216 (pipeline warmup and the per-K-step wait/sync sequence)
directions.risk: Moderate to high. More stages or a different wait-group policy can increase shared-memory footprint beyond the current 13.824 KB/block allocation and accidentally reduce the already-tight 7-CTA occupancy ceiling, so the overlap gain has to outweigh that cost.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if barrier stall stays near the current level or if extra staging raises per-block shared allocation enough to lower waves per SM without a runtime win.

directions.name: Bypass the shared epilogue scratch with a register-first BF16/vector store path
directions.hypothesis: The main tensor loop is healthier than before, but the epilogue still does a shared-memory round-trip for every 16x16 tile: `wmma::store_matrix_sync` writes to `c_shared`, the warp synchronizes, each lane rereads float values from shared, and then the kernel emits scalar BF16 stores. The detailed profile still reflects LSU-heavy behavior (`sm__inst_executed_pipe_lsu` 84.27%, `sm__mio2rf_writeback_active` 16.05%, `l1tex__data_bank_writes` 8.88%), so a register-resident unpack/pack path or wider vectorized global stores could trim epilogue-side MIO pressure and potentially recover enough shared footprint to make the 4-warp CTA easier to hide.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Epilogue LSU/MIO pressure and shared-footprint overhead from the `c_shared` round-trip
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:220-237 (WMMA store, warp syncs, shared reread, and BF16 global writeback)
directions.risk: High. WMMA accumulator layout is difficult to unpack manually, and a register-first epilogue can easily push the kernel above the current 48 registers/thread budget or introduce subtle output-ordering bugs.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if registers/thread rises materially above the current level or if the scratch-free epilogue leaves LSU and bank-write pressure essentially unchanged.
